species: 22-species synthetic subgingival microbiome community (incl. Porphyromonas gingivalis W83, Tannerella forsythia

growth_records.cite: Li et al. Nutritional pressure from serum amplifies dysbiotic features in an oral microbiome synthetic community. The ISME Journal (2026). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrag070. [PMC13140499]
growth_records.snippet: The first protocol was based on dilution rates previously used to grow oral communities with a doubling time (Td) of 6.7 h, dilution rate (D) of 0.103 h-1, and flow rate (F) of 51.56 ml h-1.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady-state community growth rate = imposed dilution rate D = 0.103 h-1, so mu = 0.103 h-1 (mu = D at steady state); Td = 6.7 h (ln2/0.103 = 6.73 h, consistent). Flow F = 51.56 mL/h (F/D = 500 mL implied working volume; assembly stated as 400 mL). Temperature 37 C and pH 7.15 +/- 0.15 automatically controlled; microaerophilic 2% O2. Serum varied 0/5/50% v/v (medium variant, does not change D). Community-level rate; per-species specific rates not reported. Acid consumption and metabolomics reported graphically only (no numeric flux).
